**Reasoning.** The hydraulic chock shield holds the immediate roof, shields the face from the goaf and advances itself and the armoured face conveyor; this is a powered roof support.

**Answer.** Powered (hydraulic) roof support / chock shield.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: In the stope shown, broken ore is left in the stope to support the walls and provide a working floor, only ~35% being drawn during mining. This is:

figure.caption: Ore-filled stope
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='40' y='20' width='100' height='160' fill='#fde9c8' stroke='#92400e'/><rect x='40' y='90' width='100' height='90' fill='#d6c0a3'/><text x='90' y='140' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>broken ore</text><text x='90' y='55' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>void</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Shrinkage stoping retains swelled broken ore as a temporary support and platform.

**Reasoning.** Because broken rock swells (~35%), that fraction is drawn off as mining rises, while the rest stays to support the walls and act as a floor — characteristic of shrinkage stoping.

**Answer.** Shrinkage stoping.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: The stope shown is mined in horizontal slices, each mined-out slice being backfilled before the next is taken. This method is:

figure.caption: Backfilled stope
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='40' y='20' width='100' height='160' fill='#fde9c8' stroke='#92400e'/><rect x='40' y='120' width='100' height='60' fill='#cbd5e1'/><text x='90' y='155' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>backfill</text><text x='90' y='90' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>ore</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Cut-and-fill replaces each mined slice with fill before the next lift.

**Reasoning.** Mining in successive horizontal lifts with backfill providing the working floor and wall support is cut-and-fill stoping, suited to weak walls and high-value ore.

**Answer.** Cut-and-fill stoping.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: The mass-mining method depicted — undercutting a large ore column so it caves under its own weight and is drawn from drawpoints below — is:

figure.caption: Caving column
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='40' y='20' width='120' height='120' fill='#d6c0a3' stroke='#92400e'/><polygon points='40,140 160,140 130,175 70,175' fill='#cbd5e1' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#1f2937'><polygon points='80,175 95,175 90,190 85,190'/><polygon points='110,175 125,175 120,190 115,190'/></g><text x='62' y='198' font-size='8'>drawpoints</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Block caving relies on gravity caving of an undercut ore column drawn from drawpoints.

**Reasoning.** Undercutting a large block so that it caves continuously and is extracted through a drawpoint grid below is the block-caving method — a low-cost mass-mining technique.

**Answer.** Block caving.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: The large block of mineral left unworked around the shaft in the plan shown is a:

figure.caption: Shaft pillar
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='10' y='10' width='180' height='180' fill='#e5e7eb'/><circle cx='100' cy='100' r='55' fill='#1f2937'/><circle cx='100' cy='100' r='10' fill='#fff' stroke='#475569'/><text x='84' y='80' font-size='8' fill='#fff'>shaft pillar</text></svg>

options: shaft (safety) pillar protecting shaft from subsidence
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A shaft pillar is unworked ground left to protect the shaft.

**Reasoning.** The ring of solid mineral around the shaft prevents subsidence damage to the shaft and surface installations; it is the shaft (safety) pillar.

**Answer.** Shaft (safety) pillar.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Roof bolts are set on a 1.5 m × 1.5 m square grid. How many bolts are needed to support a roof area of 90 m² (as shown)?

figure.caption: Bolt coverage
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='20' width='160' height='120' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#1f2937'><circle cx='50' cy='50' r='3'/><circle cx='90' cy='50' r='3'/><circle cx='130' cy='50' r='3'/><circle cx='50' cy='90' r='3'/><circle cx='90' cy='90' r='3'/><circle cx='130' cy='90' r='3'/></g><text x='30' y='152' font-size='8'>area 90 m^2, grid 1.5x1.5 m</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Number of bolts = roof area ÷ area served per bolt (spacing²).

**Working.**
$$N = \frac{90}{1.5 \times 1.5} = \frac{90}{2.25} = 40$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{40}$ bolts.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Using the per-shear output of the longwall shown (448 t per shear) with 12 shears completed in a day, compute the daily coal output (tonnes).

figure.caption: Longwall output
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='30' y='30' width='180' height='80' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='30' y='30' width='180' height='12' fill='#1f2937'/><text x='120' y='39' font-size='8' fill='#fff' text-anchor='middle'>448 t per shear</text><text x='70' y='95' font-size='8'>12 shears/day</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Daily output = output per shear × shears per day.

**Working.**
$$P = 448 \times 12 = 5376$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{5376}$ t/day.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A room-and-pillar layout has square pillars $10\ \text{m}\times10\ \text{m}$ on $20\ \text{m}\times20\ \text{m}$ centres (figure). Compute the extraction ratio.

figure.caption: Room-and-pillar plan
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 205' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='20' width='160' height='160' fill='#cbd5e1'/><g fill='#78716c' stroke='#475569'><rect x='40' y='40' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='120' y='40' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='40' y='120' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='120' y='120' width='40' height='40'/></g><text x='28' y='198' font-size='8'>pillar 10x10, centres 20x20</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $e=1-\dfrac{\text{pillar area}}{\text{tributary area}}$.

**Working.**
$$e=1-\frac{10\times10}{20\times20}=1-0.25=0.75$$

**Answer.** $0.75$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: For the tributary-area pillar shown, the vertical stress is $10\ \text{MPa}$ and the extraction ratio is $0.75$. Compute the pillar stress (MPa).

figure.caption: Tributary-area loading
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='30' y='30' width='140' height='100' fill='#cbd5e1' stroke='#475569' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/><rect x='80' y='65' width='40' height='40' fill='#78716c' stroke='#475569'/><g stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2'><line x1='60' y1='18' x2='60' y2='30'/><line x1='100' y1='18' x2='100' y2='30'/><line x1='140' y1='18' x2='140' y2='30'/></g><text x='28' y='160' font-size='8'>stress over tributary area</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\sigma_p=\dfrac{\sigma_v}{1-e}$.

**Working.**
$$\sigma_p=\frac{10}{1-0.75}=\frac{10}{0.25}=40\ \text{MPa}$$

**Answer.** $40\ \text{MPa}$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Using the Obert-Duvall formula $S_p=S_1\left(0.778+0.222\frac{W}{H}\right)$ with $S_1=30\ \text{MPa}$, $W=4\ \text{m}$ and $H=2\ \text{m}$ (figure), compute the pillar strength (MPa, two decimals).

figure.caption: Pillar width and height
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='60' y='50' width='80' height='60' fill='#78716c' stroke='#475569'/><text x='92' y='130' font-size='8'>W=4</text><text x='18' y='85' font-size='8'>H=2</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Obert-Duvall pillar strength.

**Working.**
$$S_p=30\left(0.778+0.222\times\frac{4}{2}\right)=30(0.778+0.444)=30\times1.222=36.66\ \text{MPa}$$

**Answer.** $36.66\ \text{MPa}$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A pillar has a strength of $40\ \text{MPa}$ and carries a stress of $20\ \text{MPa}$ (figure). Compute the factor of safety.

figure.caption: Pillar under load
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='70' y='40' width='60' height='80' fill='#78716c' stroke='#475569'/><g stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2'><line x1='80' y1='18' x2='80' y2='36'/><line x1='100' y1='18' x2='100' y2='36'/><line x1='120' y1='18' x2='120' y2='36'/></g><text x='28' y='145' font-size='8'>strength 40, stress 20 MPa</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $FoS=\dfrac{\text{strength}}{\text{stress}}$.

**Working.**
$$FoS=\frac{40}{20}=2$$

**Answer.** $2$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A longwall shearer cuts a $150\ \text{m}$ face with a web of $0.6\ \text{m}$ in a $3\ \text{m}$ seam of density $1.5\ \mathrm{t/m^3}$ (figure). Compute the tonnage produced per pass (t).

figure.caption: Longwall face
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='30' y='50' width='160' height='20' fill='#1f2937'/><rect x='30' y='70' width='160' height='30' fill='#a16207'/><g fill='#94a3b8' stroke='#475569'><rect x='40' y='40' width='12' height='30'/><rect x='90' y='40' width='12' height='30'/><rect x='140' y='40' width='12' height='30'/></g><text x='28' y='125' font-size='8'>face 150 m, web 0.6, h 3</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Tonnage $=$ face $\times$ web $\times$ height $\times$ density.

**Working.**
$$W=150\times0.6\times3\times1.5=405\ \text{t}$$

**Answer.** $405$ t.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A room-and-pillar panel has square pillars $8\ \text{m}\times8\ \text{m}$ on $20\ \text{m}\times20\ \text{m}$ centres (figure). Compute the recovery (%).

figure.caption: Room-and-pillar recovery
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 205' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='20' width='160' height='160' fill='#cbd5e1'/><g fill='#78716c' stroke='#475569'><rect x='40' y='40' width='32' height='32'/><rect x='120' y='40' width='32' height='32'/><rect x='40' y='120' width='32' height='32'/><rect x='120' y='120' width='32' height='32'/></g><text x='26' y='198' font-size='8'>pillar 8x8, centres 20x20</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Recovery $=\left(1-\dfrac{\text{pillar area}}{\text{tributary area}}\right)\times100$.

**Working.**
$$R=\left(1-\frac{8\times8}{20\times20}\right)\times100=(1-0.16)\times100=84\%$$

**Answer.** $84\%$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A hoisting skip lifts $10\ \text{t}$ per trip and completes $30\ \text{trips per hour}$ (figure). Compute the hoisting rate (t/hr).

figure.caption: Headframe and skip
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='60,30 120,30 100,160 80,160' fill='none' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='90' cy='40' r='12' fill='none' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='80' y='90' width='20' height='25' fill='#94a3b8' stroke='#475569'/><text x='22' y='175' font-size='8'>skip 10 t, 30 trips/hr</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Rate $=$ payload $\times$ trips per hour.

**Working.**
$$Q=10\times30=300\ \text{t/hr}$$

**Answer.** $300$ t/hr.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A development heading is advanced by $3\ \text{rounds per shift}$, each pulling $2.5\ \text{m}$ (figure). Compute the advance per shift (m).

figure.caption: Heading rounds
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='40' width='180' height='50' fill='#d6d3d1' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='90' y1='40' x2='90' y2='90' stroke='#475569' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><line x1='140' y1='40' x2='140' y2='90' stroke='#475569' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><text x='25' y='115' font-size='8'>3 rounds x 2.5 m pull</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Advance $=$ rounds $\times$ pull per round.

**Working.**
$$L=3\times2.5=7.5\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** $7.5$ m.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A stope yields $18\ \text{t}$ of ore together with $2\ \text{t}$ of wall waste (figure). Compute the dilution (%).

figure.caption: Ore with wall dilution
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='50' y='40' width='100' height='60' fill='#a16207' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='50' y='40' width='100' height='10' fill='#cbd5e1' stroke='#475569'/><text x='62' y='78' font-size='8' fill='#ffffff'>ore 18</text><text x='58' y='128' font-size='8'>wall dilution 2</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dilution $=\dfrac{\text{waste}}{\text{ore}+\text{waste}}\times100$.

**Working.**
$$D=\frac{2}{18+2}\times100=\frac{2}{20}\times100=10\%$$

**Answer.** $10\%$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: The method shown removes ore in horizontal slices and places backfill in the void after each slice. It is:

figure.caption: Cut-and-fill stoping
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='40' y='100' width='120' height='30' fill='#cbd5e1' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='40' y='70' width='120' height='30' fill='#a16207' stroke='#475569'/><text x='52' y='120' font-size='8'>backfill</text><text x='58' y='90' font-size='8' fill='#fff'>ore slice</text><text x='30' y='150' font-size='8'>fill rises with extraction</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Supported stoping methods use backfill.

**Reasoning.** Ore mined in successive horizontal slices with the void filled after each slice describes cut-and-fill stoping; the fill supports the walls and forms the next working floor.

**Answer.** Cut-and-fill stoping.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: In the layout shown, ore is blasted into sublevels and drawn off while the overlying rock caves to fill the void. The method is:

figure.caption: Sublevel caving
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 175' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g stroke='#475569'><line x1='30' y1='50' x2='170' y2='50'/><line x1='30' y1='90' x2='170' y2='90'/><line x1='30' y1='130' x2='170' y2='130'/></g><polygon points='90,40 110,40 130,150 70,150' fill='#cbd5e1' opacity='0.6'/><text x='28' y='168' font-size='8'>caved waste flows over drifts</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Caving methods let the hanging wall collapse.

**Reasoning.** Ore broken from sublevel drifts and drawn under caving waste, which fills the void, is characteristic of sublevel caving.

**Answer.** Sublevel caving.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Roof bolts are installed across a $4.8\ \text{m}$ wide roadway at $1.2\ \text{m}$ spacing, with bolts at both ribs (figure). Compute the number of bolts in one row.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Bolts $=\dfrac{\text{span}}{\text{spacing}}+1$.

**Working.**
$$N=\frac{4.8}{1.2}+1=4+1=5$$

**Answer.** $5$ bolts.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: In the figure, an undercut ore column caves under its own weight and is removed through openings at the base. These openings are:

figure.caption: Block caving draw points
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 175' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='40' y='30' width='120' height='90' fill='#cbd5e1' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#94a3b8'><polygon points='50,120 70,120 60,150'/><polygon points='90,120 110,120 100,150'/><polygon points='130,120 150,120 140,150'/></g><text x='28' y='168' font-size='8'>openings below the caving block</text></svg>

options: draw points through which caved ore is extracted
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Block caving extracts caved ore by gravity flow.

**Reasoning.** The undercut column caves and the broken ore flows down to be removed through draw points on the production level.

**Answer.** Draw points through which caved ore is extracted.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A square pillar has a $10\ \text{m}\times10\ \text{m}$ plan and is $8\ \text{m}$ high (figure). Compute its volume ($\mathrm{m^3}$).

figure.caption: Square pillar
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 175' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='60,60 120,60 150,40 90,40' fill='#9ca3af' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='60' y='60' width='60' height='70' fill='#78716c' stroke='#475569'/><polygon points='120,60 150,40 150,110 120,130' fill='#6b7280' stroke='#475569'/><text x='26' y='160' font-size='8'>plan 10x10, height 8</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $V=\text{base area}\times\text{height}$.

**Working.**
$$V=10\times10\times8=800\ \mathrm{m^3}$$

**Answer.** $800\ \mathrm{m^3}$.
